events.title: TD Summer Reading Club - Book Reporting
events.description: June 20th - August 31st. Ages 0 - 13. Summer Reading Club is here ! Register your child for the club and receive a TDSRC book reporting workbook, stickers, and more to get the most out of your summer reading. Kids will be able to report 2 books per day during the book reporting times and for  every 5 books reported they will receive an entry for a grand prize ! Find fun activities in branch all summer long and online at tdsummerreadingclub.ca . This years theme is SET SAIL! Explore the worl

events.title: Scavenger Hunt: Local Ducks
events.description: Drop by Fort York Library to find local duck species hidden around the branch and win a special 3D printed prize made in Fort York's own Digital Innovation Hub! This month's scavenger hunt features illustrations of local Toronto duck species from the Special Collections in the Arts at the Toronto Reference Library, many of which can be spotted in their full feathered glory on Toronto's waterfront.

events.title: EarlyON Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 5 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment. This program is offered in partnership with the Malvern Family Resource Centre (MFRC) . This is a drop-in program. No registration required.

events.title: EarlyON Play & Learn
events.description: This is a free, drop-in program where children and their parents or caregivers can enjoy play-based learning, creative activities, games, and stories and songs while learning about the stages of child development. Registration required upon arrival for new clients.

events.title: Halloween in July
events.description: Join us for a special Halloween themed story time. We will read stories, sing songs and get in the spooky spirit. Come dressed in costume for our spooky summer celebration. Stick around after the story time to make a mask. Visit the front desk for a one stop “trick-or-treat” and take home a pencil. Program is geared towards ages 0-6, but all ages are welcome. Drop-in. Space is limited.

events.title: Mr. Eric's Musical Adventures
events.description: Mr. Eric's Musical Adventures is a music lesson packaged in silly fun and nonsense! Through highly engaging and catchy original songs and interactive audience participation, Mr. Eric teaches rhythm, rhyming, song writing, singing, movement, and pitch, with a big boost of vocabulary to boot! While he’s spent years as a professional orchestra conductor, Mr. Eric has found a special kind of joy in sharing music and stories with young audiences! For children ages 4-8. Drop in.

events.title: Game Time

events.title: Nature Detectives
events.description: This is a nature-themed program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 6 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment.  This is a drop-in program for children ages 0-6. Co-sponsored with EarlyON, Malvern Family Resource Centre.

events.title: Movie Screening: Hoppers (2026)
events.description: Join us on a Tuesday afternoon for a fun, exciting, new movie! Feel free to bring a friend and your own snacks! Movie screening of Hoppers  (2026) G rating (acf-film.com)

events.title: Arts & Crafts

events.description: Come join us to watch Finding Dory. Bring a friend or bring your family. For Families and Children. Spaces are limited. Tuesday August 25: 2pm-4pm Please note due to allergies there will be no popcorn served.

events.title: Applefun Puppet Show: The Purple Pirate

In this 45-minute interactive program, join the Purple Pirate on the chase for the Great Purple Whale! After the program, participants will learn how to make a paper puppet to take home. Drop-in. No registration required. For children aged 3-12 and their families. Children under the age of 5 must be accompanied by a parent or caregiver during the program.

For children ages 4 and up.  No registration is required.  Building toys for younger children are available in the Children's Department.

events.title: Sunday Stories and Craft
events.description: A fun program for families to come together and enjoy stories and craft. Drop-In.

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Come celebrate your Sunday afternoons at Port Union Library! Will you colour your favourite characters or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? What about a craft?  Every Sunday come in for a surprise! This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot! No registration required.

events.title: Sunday Fun Club
events.description: Come out to our Sunday Fun Club! Get creative with a new STEM and craft activity each week. Learn new things, make stuff, meet other kids and have fun! Drop-in program, intended for ages 6-12.

events.title: STEAM Lab
events.description: Join us for our weekly STEAM Lab (Ages 6-12) , where kids can explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through fun, hands-on activities. From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.title: Snap Circuits Sundays
events.description: Want to play with electricity? Drop in for a half hour of fun with circuitry. Turn on a light, spin a motor, activate a speaker; work your way through the 100+ circuitry projects as you learn about electricity! For ages 6-12.  Parent/Caregiver must attend. Drop in program. Space is limited.

Rated PG September 4 - Ratatouille (2007). Run Time 1h 51 min. Rated G.

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

events.title: LEGO Free Play
events.description: Do you love to build and to create?  Check out Yorkville's LEGO program!  Have fun, meet friends, and build something awesome in a relaxed drop-in environment. For children ages 4 and up.  No registration is required.  Building toys for younger children are available in the Children's Department.

events.title: After School STEM Workshop

events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! For children ages 6-12. Parents are welcome to stay during the program, but please only register children. Informatio

Registration is required. Register online through the website, or call 416-393-7720 or visit the Riverdale Branch in person. Daycares and Camps, please call 416-393-7720 to inquire. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: LEGO Club
events.description: Join us after school on Thursdays for LEGO Club. Build, create and use your imagination. Drop-in, but space is limited.

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Live or automated closed captioning, sign language interpretation, intervenor services, or attendant services are available upon request. Please submit your request at least 2 weeks in advance to: tpl.ca/accessibility-events.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes, and more for children aged 0-5 with their families and/or caregivers. Older siblings are welcome! Drop-in; no registration required. Family Time occurs seasonally from October to March.

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon
events.description: Join us for Sunday crafts. Different activity each week. Sundays 12-4:30 pm. Drop-in. Live or automated closed captioning, sign language interpretation, intervenor services, or attendant services are available upon request. Please submit your request at least 2 weeks in advance to: tpl.ca/accessibility-events.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Explore different STEM-themed activities every Sunday. Drop-in, no registration required. Space is limited. Access and use of equipment will be on a first come, first serve basis. Suitable for ages 6-12.

events.title: LEGO CLUB
events.description: Are you a fan of LEGO? Join your friends to explore, create and build from your imagination or one of our challenges. Ages 4 and up are welcome. Under 4, must be accompanied by an adult, as space permits.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Sunday Build & Play
events.description: Create exciting structures using Feltro tiles and KEVA blocks. For ages 4 - 12

events.title: Sunday SNAP Circuits
events.description: Come join us and explore electricity and circuitry. Learn about circuits and gain hands on experience with SNAP Circuits! Feel the spark when you build your own circuit! For children ages 6-12. Drop in.

events.title: Curious Chaos Club
events.description: Step into The Curious Chaos Club- where creativity gets a little messy (in the best way!) Kids ages 6- 12 can build, create and let the chaos begin!

events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! This month's theme: Programming in Robotics This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Seniors Celebrating Seniors: A Musical Walk Down Memory Lane
events.description: Join Jerry and Peter for an engaging and nostalgic musical experience featuring a rich selection of beloved songs from the 1950s and 1960s. events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: Works by Asefeh Katoozian
events.description: Throughout the month of July, the works of artist Asefeh Katoozian are on display at Morningside branch. Asefeh is a multidisciplinary artist and architect with an interest in philosophy. Her work explores painting through rhythm, form, structure, and visual language, focusing on the relationship between composition and perception. events.title: Scavenger Hunt: Local Ducks
events.description: Drop by Fort York Library to find local duck species hidden around the branch and win a special 3D printed prize made in Fort York's own Digital Innovation Hub! This month's scavenger hunt features illustrations of local Toronto duck species from the Special Collections in the Arts at the Toronto Reference Library, many of which can be spotted in their full feathered glory on Toronto's waterfront.

events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! For children age 9+. events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop
events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Milk with Boba. Participants will learn to prepare and use the classic ingredients​ including milk, molasses and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally.

events.title: Digital Photography I: The Basics
events.description: Come and up your photography game! events.title: Let's Learn About Computer Hardware

events.title: Japanese Friendship Bracelet Making for Kids!

events.description: Join us to observe the Sun safely using telescopes fitted with certified solar filters. This program will be run by two PhD students studying astronomy at the University of Toronto. They'll be happy to answer your 'burning' questions about the sun and space. For all ages! events.title: Raíces — Classical & Latin American Guitar
events.description: Toronto-based guitarist Karla García invites audiences on a musical journey from the elegance of the Baroque era to the rich musical traditions of Latin America. Featuring works by Bach, Dilermando Reis, Víctor Jara, Luiz Bonfá, and Chilean composers Antonio Restucci, alongside original compositions from her upcoming debut album, this concert celebrates the timeless voice of the classical guitar—intimate, soulful, and deeply human.

events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union Library's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot.  No registration required.

events.title: Introduction to 3D Printing
events.description: Learn how 3D printers work and how you can print at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class includes hands-on instruction with Prusa Slicer, the software used to prepare your 3D object file for printing on a Prusa MK3S+ 3D printer. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing a

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Sunday International Chess Club
events.description: Join the Sunday Chess Club, as we play chess every other Sunday. Players of all strengths and skill level are welcomed to participate in the club. Chess introduction lessons are available for players that are new to the game. Sets are provided. The program is open to all ages 7 years and above.

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find
